summary: Closed unmerged PR attempting to match grInishie1_801FA9B4, with additional local matching edits in gryorster.c and lbsnap.c. The author comment, "Still haven't hard reverted. My mistake again," suggests the branch contained unwanted or not-yet-reverted changes, so this should be treated as a non-landed experiment rather than accepted project direction.
what_changed: In src/melee/gr/grinishie1.c, added a static s16 array grI1_803E48C8 containing stage-related block/index data.

Added /GrI1.dat string data, a grInishie1_StageData typedef wrapping StageData plus a report format buffer, and an initialized grI1_803E4950 object containing INISHIE1 stage metadata and the OSReport format string.
Added grI1_803E49A8 as the source filename string "grinishie1.c".
Changed grInishie1_801FA9B4 to derive its StageCallbacks pointer through pointer arithmetic from grI1_803E48C8 plus 0x2c rather than directly taking &grI1_803E48F4[arg0].
Changed the missing-gobj OSReport call in grInishie1_801FA9B4 to load both the format string and filename through newly modeled data symbols.
In grInishie1_801FBC4C, split a nested DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP argument into a temporary HSD_JObj* jobj2 before calling the macro.
In src/melee/gr/gryorster.c, rewrote grYorster_80202428 loop structure with an explicit temporary, manual increment, pointer/integer casts, and a temporary f32 newValue.
In src/melee/lb/lbsnap.c, rewrote lbSnap_8001DF20 to use a descPtr temporary, an offset temporary, and field writes through the descriptor pointer.
smart_moves: Modeled nearby rodata/data layout instead of only changing code shape: grInishie1_801FA9B4 was adjusted to reference report strings and callbacks as the compiler likely did through surrounding data.
Used a wrapper struct around StageData to explain why the OSReport format string can be addressed as data adjacent to stage metadata.
Introduced temporaries such as HSD_JObj* jobj2, f32 newValue, offset, and descPtr to influence register allocation and expression ordering, a common decomp matching tactic.

Tried pointer arithmetic from an earlier data symbol plus a byte offset to reproduce callback-table addressing, which can be useful when the original object layout has not been fully separated into clean C declarations.
decomp_lessons: For stage files, matching one function may require reconstructing adjacent data objects, strings, and table layout, not just the function body.
Directly referencing a named array such as grI1_803E48F4 may not match if the original code addressed the same memory through a different base symbol or aggregate layout.
OSReport calls often expose rodata placement and can require named file strings and format strings to match codegen and relocations.
Macro-heavy calls such as DOBJ_CLEAR_LOOP may need intermediate locals to obtain the original instruction order.
Be cautious about submitting branches with unrelated local matching experiments; this PR was closed quickly with an author note implying a failed or incomplete revert.
Because the PR was not merged, none of its tactics should be assumed accepted without retesting against current master.
naming_conventions: Stage-local data symbols used the grI1_ prefix followed by address-like suffixes, e.g. grI1_803E48C8, grI1_803E4950, and grI1_803E49A8.
Function names followed existing stage naming such as grInishie1_801FA9B4 and grInishie1_801FBC4C.
The added wrapper typedef used a descriptive stage-scoped name, grInishie1_StageData, while preserving the embedded StageData field name stage_data.
Temporary names in the diff were pragmatic matching names such as descPtr, offset, temp, newValue, and jobj2.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use of an explicit casted pointer expression: (StageCallbacks*) ((char*) grI1_803E48C8 + 0x2c) + arg0.
Replaced direct string literals in OSReport with loads from modeled data fields and separate filename string symbols.
Split complex field/macro expressions into local variables to influence load order and register usage.
Changed a normal for-loop increment into manual increment inside the loop body in grYorster_80202428.
Compared pointers through integer casts in grYorster_80202428: reading x1C through an s32 temporary and comparing item_gobj as a u32.
Introduced descriptor and offset locals in lbSnap_8001DF20 so writes and the lb_8001C4A8 call go through descPtr rather than direct global field expressions.

review_feedback.text: Still haven't hard reverted. My mistake again
review_feedback.interpretation: The PR likely included unwanted unreverted changes or was opened from a dirty branch; it was closed unmerged shortly after creation.

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for a merged version of grInishie1_801FA9B4 to see which data-layout tactic was accepted.
Search for subsequent cleanup or revert PRs by DavidWeaverAudio around grinishie1, gryorster, or lbsnap.
Verify whether grI1_803E4950 or the grInishie1_StageData wrapper exists in current master before reusing this layout.
Compare current grYorster_80202428 and lbSnap_8001DF20 against this PR to determine whether these unrelated matching edits were ever landed elsewhere.
